query_normalized: predicting insect pollinator networks from floral trait data biology
rationale: This paper belongs in the literature review because it studies the exact empirical population (plant-pollinator networks) and analyzes the structural properties and robustness of these networks, satisfying criteria (a) and (e) by providing foundational evidence on the mechanisms governing the very networks the user aims to predict, even though it focuses on temporal dynamics rather than floral traits specifically.
